title: Global Fossil Fuel Production and Consumption - Power BI Dashboard
description:
Do you know that:
The Last Decade Was the Hottest in 125,000 Years?
The 10 warmest years in the historical record have all occurred since 2010?

Lets find out why...
Fossil fuels are formed from the decomposition of buried carbon-based organisms that died millions of years ago. They create carbon-rich deposits that are extracted and burned for energy. They are non-renewable and currently supply around 80% of the world’s energy. They been a fundamental driver of the technological, social, economic and development progress.
Fossil fuels (coal, oil, gas) have, and continue to, play a dominant role in global energy systems.
But they also come with several negative impacts. When burned they produce carbon dioxide (CO2) and are the largest driver of global climate change. They are also a major contributor to local air pollution, which is estimated to linked to millions of premature deaths each year.
As low-carbon sources of energy – nuclear and renewables – become readily available, the world needs to rapidly transition away from fossil fuels.
This projects provides detailed insights on fossil fuel production and consumption across the globe. This report will help identify the countries that are major consumers of fossil fuel which should take steps to move towards the renewable energy sources like wind, solar, nuclear fusion to help minimize the global warming and pollution to make the earth a better place to live.

Do you know that: The Last Decade Was the Hottest in 125,000 Years? The 10 warmest years in the historical record have all occurred since 2010? Lets find out why... Fossil fuels are formed from the decomposition of buried carbon-based organisms that died millions of years ago. They create carbon-rich deposits that are extracted and burned for energy. They are non-renewable and currently supply around 80% of the world’s energy. They been a fundamental driver of the technological, social, economic and development progress. Fossil fuels (coal, oil, gas) have, and continue to, play a dominant role in global energy systems. But they also come with several negative impacts. When burned they produce carbon dioxide (CO2) and are the largest driver of global climate change. They are also a major contributor to local air pollution, which is estimated to linked to millions of premature deaths each year. As low-carbon sources of energy – nuclear and renewables – become readily available, the world needs to rapidly transition away from fossil fuels. This projects provides detailed insights on fossil fuel production and consumption across the globe. This report will help identify the countries that are major consumers of fossil fuel which should take steps to move towards the renewable energy sources like wind, solar, nuclear fusion to help minimize the global warming and pollution to make the earth a better place to live.

Tips For Effective Dashboard

Global Fossil Fuel Production and Consumption - Power BI Dashboard
Do you know that: The Last Decade Was the Hottest in 125,000 Years? The 10 warmest years in the historical record have all occurred since 2010? Lets find out why... Fossil fuels are formed from the decomposition of buried carbon-based organisms that died millions of years ago. They create carbon-rich deposits that are extracted and burned for energy. They are non-renewable and currently supply around 80% of the world’s energy. They been a fundamental driver of the technological, social, economic and development progress. Fossil fuels (coal, oil, gas) have, and continue to, play a dominant role in global energy systems. But they also come with several negative impacts. Step Name:Importing Data
Detailed Instructions:

Raw Dataset Link - https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/shawkatsujon/worldwide-fuel-production-and-consumption

https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/statchaitya/country-to-continent

There are two dataset links which has one table each. I have downloaded both the dataset from Kaggle and saved in my system as xlsx files.
1.Open Power BI.
2.Click Get Data.
3.Choose Excel Workbook.
4.Choose the Fuel Production & Consumption excel file from downloads.
5.Click the checkbox next to excel file and select Transform data , a power query editor will be opened with Fuel Production & Consumption data.

Insight: 

How to Import Excel Data

Global Fossil Fuel Production and Consumption - Power BI Dashboard
Do you know that: The Last Decade Was the Hottest in 125,000 Years? The 10 warmest years in the historical record have all occurred since 2010? Lets find out why... Fossil fuels are formed from the decomposition of buried carbon-based organisms that died millions of years ago. They create carbon-rich deposits that are extracted and burned for energy. They are non-renewable and currently supply around 80% of the world’s energy. They been a fundamental driver of the technological, social, economic and development progress. Fossil fuels (coal, oil, gas) have, and continue to, play a dominant role in global energy systems. But they also come with several negative impacts. When burned they produce carbon dioxide (CO2) and are the largest driver of global climate change. They are also a major contributor to local air pollution, which is estimated to linked to millions of premature deaths each year. As low-carbon sources of energy – nuclear and renewables – become readily available, the world needs to rapidly transition away from fossil fuels. This projects provides detailed insights on fossil fuel production and consumption across the globe. This report will help identify the countries that are major consumers of fossil fuel which should take steps to move towards the renewable energy sources like wind, solar, nuclear fusion to help minimize the global warming and pollution to make the earth a better place to live.
Step Name:Transforming the Data in Power Query Editor
Detailed Instructions:
1. Click on the drop down button in Year Column and Select Years from 2001 to 2019, and Entity column drop down and unselect 'World', those are the years we are going to analyze. 

2. Rename Entity column to Country
3. Rename the table 'Fuel Production and Consumption' to 'Fuel'

4.Select Columns Coal Production per capita, Coal Consumption per capita, Gas Production per capita, Gas Consumption per capita, Oil Production per Capita, Oil Consumption per Capita
5. Right Click on any selected column title.
6. Click Remove Columns

7. Data Conversion
i) Select Add Custom Column from Add Column Ribbon and use the formula below to convert Gas Production metrics from cubic meter (m3) to kwh and rename the custom column as Gas Production (kwh)
Gas:
1 m3 = 11.363 kwh
https://myboiler.com/gas-volume-m3-to-kwh-calculator/

ii) Do the above step for Gas Consumption as well.
iii) Repeat the same step for Converting Coal Production and Coal Consumption to convert metrics from Ton to kwh using the below formula
Coal:
1 Ton = 8141 kwh
https://www.convertunits.com/from/tonne+of+coal+equivalent/to/kWh
iv) Repeat the above step for Oil Production and Oil Consumption to covert metrics from cubic meters to kwh for oil using the below formula.
Oil:
1 m3 = 10241.378  kwh
https://www.unitconverters.net/volume/cubic-meter-to-barrel-oil.htm
https://www.unitjuggler.com/convert-energy-from-boe-to-kWh.html

v)Change all these custom columns datatype to Decimal Number
8. Choose the Coal Production(ton) ,Coal consumption (ton), Gas Production (m3), Gas Consumption (m3), Oil Production (m3), Oil Consumption(m3)  and Right click and Remove these Columns
9. Add 2 custom columns for Total Fuel Production and Consumptions using the below formula and data type as Decimal Number.
Total Fuel Production = Coal Production(kwh)+Gas Production(kwh)+Oil Production(kwh)
Total Fuel Consumption = Coal Consumption(kwh)+Gas Consumption(kwh)+Oil Consumption(kwh)

10. Now to change ‘year’ column to date format, first Duplicate the Year Column and rename it to Year Source,
11.Then change Original year column data type to text. To do that , in the home ribbon, under transform section there is datatype button and click it and select text.
12. Sort the year column to ascending again (by clicking arrow next to column name and select ascending) , now in the transform ribbon click datatype and select ‘date’.
13. Now the year is in date format, change that column name to ‘Date’.
14. Right Click on the Date Column and Transform-> Year->End of the Year.
15.Now the data in this table is ready to load, click on file and select close & apply. You will see that table loaded into power bi.
16. Repeat the steps 2-4 from (Step 1 Importing Data) to import Country_Continent dataset Excel file.
17. From power query editor, select Country, continent and subregion columns by pressing Ctrl key and Right click and click Remove Other Columns
18. Select Merge Queries from Home in the ribbon and Choose Continent_Country table and Fuel Table and select Country columns from both the tables and Choose Left Outer Join. Click Ok.
19. Fuel Table will be added with a new column-> Expand that column by clicking on Expand button and Select Continent and Country to Expand and hit ok.
20. Rename Both the columns as Country_New and Continent. Right click each column and unselect Null values from both the columns. Then Click Remove Empty from Continent column. Click Close and Apply.

Insight: 

Clean, Transform and Load Data

Global Fossil Fuel Production and Consumption - Power BI Dashboard
Do you know that: The Last Decade Was the Hottest in 125,000 Years? The 10 warmest years in the historical record have all occurred since 2010? Lets find out why... Fossil fuels are formed from the decomposition of buried carbon-based organisms that died millions of years ago. They create carbon-rich deposits that are extracted and burned for energy. They are non-renewable and currently supply around 80% of the world’s energy. They been a fundamental driver of the technological, social, economic and development progress. Fossil fuels (coal, oil, gas) have, and continue to, play a dominant role in global energy systems. But they also come with several negative impacts. When burned they produce carbon dioxide (CO2) and are the largest driver of global climate change. They are also a major contributor to local air pollution, which is estimated to linked to millions of premature deaths each year. As low-carbon sources of energy – nuclear and renewables – become readily available, the world needs to rapidly transition away from fossil fuels. This projects provides detailed insights on fossil fuel production and consumption across the globe. This report will help identify the countries that are major consumers of fossil fuel which should take steps to move towards the renewable energy sources like wind, solar, nuclear fusion to help minimize the global warming and pollution to make the earth a better place to live.
Step Name:Adding Calendar and Measures Table
Detailed Instructions:

1. Creating Calendar Table. On Power BI desktop under Modeling click on new table button. Type the Dax formula below in the Dax formula bar, click on the check mark, and save.

Calendar = FILTER(CALENDAR(DATE(2001, 1, 1), DATE(2020, 1, 1)), DAY([Date]) = 31 && MONTH([Date]) = 12)
Year = FORMAT('Calendar'[Date], "yyyy")

2.Creating Measure Table
From the "Home" tab in ribbon, click "Enter Data" and just rename the blank table to "MeasuresForFuel"  and click OK. Click on Measures table in the fields section and then right click and choose 'new measure' and enter 1 measure formula from below and click tick next to it to create that measure. Right click on the measure table to create another measure and repeat the same steps until all the below measures are created in "MeasuresForFuel" table.

Measures:
1. Coal Prod = AVERAGE(Fuel[COAL Production (kwh)])
2. Coal Cons = AVERAGE(Fuel[COAL Consumption (kwh)])
3. Gas Prod = AVERAGE(Fuel[GAS Production(kwh)])
4. Gas Cons = AVERAGE(Fuel[GAS Consumption (kwh)])
5. Oil Prod = AVERAGE(Fuel[OIL Production (kwh)])
6. Oil Cons = AVERAGE(Fuel[OIL Consumption (kwh)])
7. FuelProd = AVERAGE(Fuel[Total FUEL Production ])
8. FuelCons = AVERAGE(Fuel[Total FUEL Consumption])

9. Coal Production YOY = 
VAR CoalProd_CY=AVERAGE('Fuel'[COAL Production (kwh)])
VAR CoalProd_PrevY = CALCULATE(AVERAGE(Fuel[COAL Production (kwh)]),SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Calendar'[Date]))
RETURN
IF(ISBLANK(CoalProd_PrevY),0,CALCULATE(CoalProd_CY-CoalProd_PrevY))

10. Coal Consumption YOY = 
VAR CoalCons_CY=AVERAGE('Fuel'[COAL Consumption (kwh)])
VAR CoalCons_PrevY = CALCULATE(AVERAGE(Fuel[COAL Consumption (kwh)]),SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Calendar'[Date]))
RETURN
IF(ISBLANK(CoalCons_PrevY),0,CALCULATE(CoalCons_CY-CoalCons_PrevY))

11.Coal(P) = 
VAR CoalProd_CY=AVERAGE('Fuel'[COAL Production (kwh)])
VAR CoalProd_PrevY = CALCULATE(AVERAGE(Fuel[COAL Production (kwh)]),SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Calendar'[Date]))
RETURN
IF(ISBLANK(CoalProd_PrevY),0,DIVIDE((CoalProd_CY-CoalProd_PrevY), CoalProd_PrevY))

12.Coal(C) = 
VAR CoalCons_CY=AVERAGE('Fuel'[COAL Consumption (kwh)])
VAR CoalCons_PrevY = CALCULATE(AVERAGE(Fuel[COAL Consumption (kwh)]),SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Calendar'[Date]))
RETURN
IF(ISBLANK(CoalCons_PrevY),0,DIVIDE((CoalCons_CY-CoalCons_PrevY), CoalCons_PrevY))

13.Gas Production YOY = 
VAR GASProd_CY=Average('Fuel'[GAS Production(kwh)])
VAR GASProd_PrevY = CALCULATE(Average(Fuel[GAS Production(kwh)]),SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Calendar'[Date]))
RETURN
IF(ISBLANK(GASProd_PrevY),0,CALCULATE(GASProd_CY-GASProd_PrevY))

14.Gas Consumption YOY = 
VAR GasCons_CY=AVERAGE('Fuel'[GAS Consumption (kwh)])
VAR GasCons_PrevY = CALCULATE(AVERAGE(Fuel[GAS Consumption (kwh)]),SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Calendar'[Date]))
RETURN
IF(ISBLANK(GasCons_PrevY),0,CALCULATE(GasCons_CY-GasCons_PrevY))

15.Gas(P) = 
VAR GasProd_CY=AVERAGE('Fuel'[GAS Production(kwh)])
VAR GasProd_PrevY = CALCULATE(AVERAGE(Fuel[GAS Production(kwh)]),SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Calendar'[Date]))
RETURN
IF(ISBLANK(GasProd_PrevY),0,DIVIDE(GasProd_CY-GasProd_PrevY, GasProd_PrevY))

16.Gas(C) = 
VAR GasCons_CY=Average('Fuel'[GAS Consumption (kwh)])
VAR GasCons_PrevY = CALCULATE(Average(Fuel[GAS Consumption (kwh)]),SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Calendar'[Date]))
RETURN
IF(ISBLANK(GasCons_PrevY),0,DIVIDE(GasCons_CY-GasCons_PrevY, GasCons_PrevY))

17.Oil Production YOY = 
VAR OilProd_CY=AVERAGE('Fuel'[OIL Production (kwh)])
VAR OilProd_PrevY = CALCULATE(AVERAGE(Fuel[OIL Production (kwh)]),SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Calendar'[Date]))
RETURN
IF(ISBLANK(OilProd_PrevY),0,CALCULATE(OilProd_CY-OilProd_PrevY))

18. Oil Consumption YOY = 
VAR OilCons_CY=AVERAGE('Fuel'[OIL Consumption (kwh)])
VAR OilCons_PrevY = CALCULATE(AVERAGE(Fuel[OIL Consumption (kwh)]),SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Calendar'[Date]))
RETURN
IF(ISBLANK(OilCons_PrevY),0,CALCULATE(OilCons_CY-OilCons_PrevY))

19.Oil(P) = 
VAR OilProd_CY=AVERAGE('Fuel'[OIL Production (kwh)])
VAR OilProd_PrevY = CALCULATE(AVERAGE(Fuel[OIL Production (kwh)]),SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Calendar'[Date]))
RETURN
IF(ISBLANK(OilProd_PrevY),0,DIVIDE(OilProd_CY-OilProd_PrevY, OilProd_PrevY))

20.Oil(C) = 
VAR OilCons_CY=AVERAGE('Fuel'[OIL Consumption (kwh)])
VAR OilCons_PrevY = CALCULATE(AVERAGE(Fuel[OIL Consumption (kwh)]),SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Calendar'[Date]))
RETURN
IF(ISBLANK(OilCons_PrevY),0,DIVIDE(OilCons_CY-OilCons_PrevY, OilCons_PrevY))

21.FUELProdYOY = 
VAR FuelProd_CY=AVERAGE('Fuel'[Total FUEL Production ])
VAR FuelProd_PrevY = CALCULATE(AVERAGE(Fuel[Total FUEL Production ]),DATEADD('Calendar'[Date],-1,YEAR))
RETURN
IF(ISBLANK(FuelProd_PrevY),0,CALCULATE(FuelProd_CY-FuelProd_PrevY))

22.FUELConsYOY = 
VAR FuelCons_CY=AVERAGE('Fuel'[Total FUEL Consumption])
VAR FuelCons_PrevY = CALCULATE(AVERAGE(Fuel[Total FUEL Consumption]),DATEADD('Calendar'[Date],-1,YEAR))
RETURN
IF(ISBLANK(FuelCons_PrevY),0,CALCULATE(FuelCons_CY-FuelCons_PrevY))

23.Fuel (P) YOY = 
VAR FuelProd_CY=AVERAGE('Fuel'[Total FUEL Production ])
VAR FuelProd_PrevY = CALCULATE(AVERAGE(Fuel[Total FUEL Production ]),SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Calendar'[Date]))
RETURN
IF(ISBLANK(FuelProd_PrevY),0,DIVIDE(FuelProd_CY-FuelProd_PrevY, FuelProd_PrevY))

24.Fuel (C) YOY = 
VAR FuelCons_CY=AVERAGE('Fuel'[Total FUEL Consumption])
VAR FuelCons_PrevY = CALCULATE(AVERAGE(Fuel[Total FUEL Consumption]),SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Calendar'[Date]))
RETURN
IF(ISBLANK(FuelCons_PrevY),0,DIVIDE(FuelCons_CY-FuelCons_PrevY, FuelCons_PrevY))

25.COALPROD INDICATOR = 
SWITCH(
    TRUE(), [Coal(P)] > 0,
    UNICHAR(9650), UNICHAR(9660))

26.COALCONSUMP INDICATOR = 
SWITCH(
    TRUE(), [Coal(C)] > 0,
    UNICHAR(9650), UNICHAR(9660))

27.GASPROD INDICATOR = 
SWITCH(
    TRUE(), [Gas(P)] > 0,
    UNICHAR(9650), UNICHAR(9660))

28.GASCONSUMP INDICATOR = 
SWITCH(
    TRUE(), [Gas(C)] > 0,
    UNICHAR(9650), UNICHAR(9660))

29.OILPROD INDICATOR = 
SWITCH(
    TRUE(), [Oil(P)] > 0,
    UNICHAR(9650), UNICHAR(9660))

30.OILCONSUMP INDICATOR = 
SWITCH(
    TRUE(), [Oil(C)] > 0,
    UNICHAR(9650), UNICHAR(9660))

31.FUELPROD INDICATOR = 
SWITCH(
    TRUE(), [Fuel (P) YOY] > 0,
    UNICHAR(9650), UNICHAR(9660))

32.FUELCONSUMP INDICATOR = 
SWITCH(
    TRUE(), [Fuel (C) YOY] > 0,
    UNICHAR(9650), UNICHAR(9660))

Insight: 

Create Calendar Table

Creating Measures using DAX

Global Fossil Fuel Production and Consumption - Power BI Dashboard
Do you know that: The Last Decade Was the Hottest in 125,000 Years? The 10 warmest years in the historical record have all occurred since 2010? Lets find out why... Fossil fuels are formed from the decomposition of buried carbon-based organisms that died millions of years ago. They create carbon-rich deposits that are extracted and burned for energy. They are non-renewable and currently supply around 80% of the world’s energy. They been a fundamental driver of the technological, social, economic and development progress. Fossil fuels (coal, oil, gas) have, and continue to, play a dominant role in global energy systems. But they also come with several negative impacts. When burned they produce carbon dioxide (CO2) and are the largest driver of global climate change. They are also a major contributor to local air pollution, which is estimated to linked to millions of premature deaths each year. As low-carbon sources of energy – nuclear and renewables – become readily available, the world needs to rapidly transition away from fossil fuels. This projects provides detailed insights on fossil fuel production and consumption across the globe. This report will help identify the countries that are major consumers of fossil fuel which should take steps to move towards the renewable energy sources like wind, solar, nuclear fusion to help minimize the global warming and pollution to make the earth a better place to live.
Step Name:Data Modeling
Detailed Instructions:

1. From modeling tab on the ribbon Create new one to many relatioship from CountryContinent table to Fuel table and  Calendar table to Fuel Table. For that you need to drag 'Date' field from 'Calendar' table to 'Date' field in 'Fuel table' and drag 'Country' field from 'CountryContinent' table to 'Country' field in Fuel table.

Insight: 

Data Modeling

Step Name:Adding Canvas Background
Detailed Instructions:

1. From the report view, go to Visualization pane -> Format-> Canvas Background-> Browse the image used for watermark.
2. Choose Transparency as 30%
3. Change Image Fit as 'Fit'

Insight: 

Easy Canvas Background

Step Name:Title and Logos
Detailed Instructions:

Colaberry Logo -
1.From the Insert tab , click on the image button and select the Colaberry logo and click open.
2. In the format pane, go to general and enter the bellow properties.
Height=115
Width=158
Horizontal= 12
Vertical =2
Title=off
Effects->Background=on, Transparency=0%
Fuel Logo -
1.From the Insert tab , click on the image button and select the Fuel logo and click open.
2. In the format pane, go to general and enter the bellow properties.
Height=115
Width=103
Horizontal = 170
Vertical =0
Title=off
Effects->Background=on, Transparency=0%
Project Title-
1. From the Insert tab, click on the text box button, click inside the box and enter the title for the project as below
Title='Global Fossil Fuel Production and Consumption'
Font= Calibri, center aligned,
Color=#252423
size=36
2. In the format pane, go to general and enter the bellow properties.
Height=132
Width=737
Horizontal = 272
Vertical =0
Title=off
Effects->Background=on, Transparency=0%

Insight: 

Adding Images

Step Name:Year Slicer
Detailed Instructions:

1. From the visualizations pane, click on the Slicer and drag 'year' column from calendar table to values field of slicer.
2. In the format->general section, set
Outline Color=#605E5C
Outline Weight=1
Responsive=On
Height=115
Width=271
X Position = 1009
Y Position =0
Title=off
effects-background=on, transparency=0%
3.  In the format->
Set Slider to On with Color=#1A1A1A.
Set Title to On, Title Text "Year", Font Color = Black Background Color= White, Alignment=Center, Text Size=16, Bold =On, Font family=Calibri
Set Background to On, with Color =#FFFFFF and Transparency = 0%.

Insight: 

Creating Slicers

They are non-renewable and currently supply around 80% of the world’s energy. They been a fundamental driver of the technological, social, economic and development progress. Fossil fuels (coal, oil, gas) have, and continue to, play a dominant role in global energy systems. But they also come with several negative impacts. When burned they produce carbon dioxide (CO2) and are the largest driver of global climate change. They are also a major contributor to local air pollution, which is estimated to linked to millions of premature deaths each year. As low-carbon sources of energy – nuclear and renewables – become readily available, the world needs to rapidly transition away from fossil fuels. This projects provides detailed insights on fossil fuel production and consumption across the globe. This report will help identify the countries that are major consumers of fossil fuel which should take steps to move towards the renewable energy sources like wind, solar, nuclear fusion to help minimize the global warming and pollution to make the earth a better place to live.
Step Name:Main KPI Cards
Detailed Instructions:

Fuel KPI
1. From visualizations click Card visualization twice, drag 'FuelProd' for first card and 'FuelCons' for second card from 'MeasuresForFuel' to values fields.
2. For both the KPI cards, In the format->visual -> callout value, set font Style = Arial, size=20, color= #F19840, display units =Auto, category label= off
3. For 1st KPI card(Fuel Prod) , In the format->general, set
Height=113, Width=136, Horizontal=0, Vertical=175, Title=Off
Effects-background=on, color=#E6E6E6, transparency=16%
4.  For 2nd KPI card (Fuel Cons) , In the format->general, set
Height=113, Width=136, Horizontal=136, Vertical=174, Title=Off
Effects-background=on, color=#E6E6E6, transparency=16%

Coal KPI
1. For Coal KPI, copy the above created card and paste twice and drag 'CoalProd' and 'CoalCons' from 'MeasuresForFuel' to fields for each KPIs.
2. For Left KPI card (CoalProd) , In the format->general, set
Height=113, Width=136, Horizontal=0, Vertical=318
3. For right KPI card (CoalCons), In the format->general, set
Height=113, Width=136, Horizontal=136, Vertical=318

Gas KPI
1. For Gas KPI, copy the above created card and paste twice and drag 'GasProd' and 'GasCons' from 'MeasuresForFuel' to fields for each KPIs.
2. For Left KPI card (GasProd) , In the format->general, set
Height=113, Width=136, Horizontal=0, Vertical=459
3. For right KPI card (GasCons), In the format->general, set
Height=113, Width=136, Horizontal=136, Vertical=459

Oil KPI
1. For Oil KPI, copy the above created card and paste twice and drag 'OilProd' and 'OilCons' from 'MeasuresForFuel' to fields for each KPIs.
2. For Left KPI card (OilProd) , In the format->general, set
Height=113, Width=136, Horizontal=0, Vertical=605
3. For right KPI card (OilCons), In the format->general, set
Height=113, Width=136, Horizontal=136, Vertical=605

Set visual borders for all 8 main KPI cards to On and Color = #808080

Above KPI cards, Include text boxes for Titles for these KPIs.
1. ProductionVSConsumption - Text = 'ProductionvsConsumption', Font Style = Calibri, Size=14, Center allignment, Height = 32, Width =271, Horizontal =0, Vertical =116, Effect-> Background= On, Color = #983800.
2. Fuel - Copy Paste above text box and replace text with 'Fuel', Height = 32, Width =271, Horizontal =0, Vertical =143
3. Coal - Copy Paste above text box and replace text with 'Coal', Height = 32, Width =271, Horizontal =0, Vertical =286
4. Gas - Copy Paste above text box and replace text with 'Gas', Height = 32, Width =271, Horizontal =0, Vertical =431
5. Oil - Copy Paste above text box and replace text with 'Oil', Height = 32, Width =271, Horizontal =0, Vertical =573

Step Name:Fuel Production & Consumption by Year (Line and Clustered Column Chart - Top Left)
Detailed Instructions:

From the visualizations pane select Line and Clustered Column Chart.
Fill up fields 
X- axis = Year,
Column Y-Axis = FuelProd, FuelCons, 
Line Y-Axis = Coal(P), Coal(C), Gas(P), Gas(C), Oil(P), Oil(C)
Tooltips = FuelProd, FuelCons, Fuel(P)YOY, FUEL(C)YOY, CoalProd ,CoalCons, Coal(P), Coal(C), GasProd, GasCons, Gas(P), Gas(C), OilProd, OilCons, Oil(P), Oil(C).
In Format-> Visual->
X-axis: Type = Continuous, Values: Font=Calibri (12),color=black, Title - off .
Y-axis: Values: Font=Calibri (12),color=black, Title - off, Display Units=Auto. 
Secondary Y-axis: Values: Font=Calibri (12), color=black.
Legend: Position=Top left, Style= Marker (Circle default), Match Line Color=On, Text: Font=Calibri (12).
Gridlines=off.
Columns: Color-> FuelProd= #F19840 , FuelCons= #894821 
Lines: line style= solid, join type= round, stroke width= 3px.
Colors: Coal(P)= #009792, Coal(C)=#005E5B, Gas(P)=#F71E6A, Gas(C)= #A6053D, Oil(P)= #F300F3, Oil(C) = #7E057E.
Markers= Off. 
Data Labels= Off 
Series Labels = Off
In Format -> General:
Properties, Size, Height=300, Width=505, Position, Horizontal= 270, Vertical=115. Title=On, Text=Year, Font=Calibri(15), Text color= #FFFFFF, Background color=#983800, Horizontal alignment=Center, Effects: Background=On, Background color=#E6E6E6, Transparency=20%.
Tooltips: On, Font =Calibri, Size = 10, Label Color = #C8C8C8, Value Color =#FFFFFF.

Step Name:Production Vs Consumption (Waterfall Chart)
Detailed Instructions:

1. Before going to waterfall chart, Create few measures as mentioned below. Create the following 4 measures like we created other measures inside MeasuresForFuel table.
Coal(PvsC) = ABS([Coal(P)])-ABS([Coal(C)])
Gas(PvsC) = ABS([Gas(P)])-ABS([Gas(C)])
Oil(PvsC) = ABS([Oil(P)])-ABS([Oil(C)])
Fuel(PvsC) = ABS(MeasuresForFUEL[Fuel (P) YOY])- ABS(MeasuresForFUEL[Fuel (C) YOY])
2.  Next, create a measure description table.
3.  On the Power BI Desktop Home ribbon, Click on Enter data, name the first column header as ID and the second column header as PvsC and Name the table as Prod vs Cons.

4. Create a Measure called Variance of PvsC% to display the difference of Production and Consumption on the Waterfall chart

Now, Create Waterfall Chart using the below steps
1. Click on an empty space on the canvas, and from the visualizations pane select Waterfall Chart.
2. Drag Prod vs Cons to the category and Variance of PvsC% on Column y-axis.
3. Drag all the Tooltips = Fuel(PvsC), Coal(PvsC), Gas(PvsC), Oil(PvsC), FuelProd, FuelCons, Fuel(P)YOY, FUEL(C)YOY, CoalProd ,CoalCons, Coal(P), Coal(C), GasProd, GasCons, Gas(P), Gas(C), OilProd, OilCons, Oil(P), Oil(C),
4. In the Formatting Tab->
X-axis: On, Font =Calibri, size = 12, Color = #000000,Title = Off
Y-axis: On, Font = Calibri, size = 12, Color = #000000. Title: Off. 
Legend: On, Position = Top left,Text: Font = Calibri, size =12, Color =#000000. Title: Off.
Gridlines: Horizontal: Off.
Column Color = Increase #1AAB40, Decrease #F82A1B, Show total column = Off. 
Data labels: On, Horizontal. Values = Font Calibri, Size = 12, Color = #000000.
5. In the Formatting Tab->
General->Properties->Size: Height = 305, Width = 335, Position: Horizontal = 272, Vertical =415, 
Title: Production Vs Consumption, Font = Calibri, Size = 14, Color = #FFFFFF, Background= #983800 , Center, 
Effect: Background Color= #E6E6E6,Trasparency = 20%. 
Tooltips: On, Font =Calibri, Size = 10, Label Color = #C8C8C8, Value Color =#FFFFFF.

Insight: 

Create and Format Waterfall Chart

 minimum color=#F2C090, middle color=#F28110, Maximum color=#813F00

5. For Format  -> General:
    Properties, Size, Height=305, Width=334, Position, Horizontal= 945, Vertical=415.            Title=On, Text=Global Fuel, Font=Calibri (15), Text color= #FFFFFF, Background                color=#983800 , Horizontal alignment=Center

   Properties: Height=32, width=132, horizontal=982,vertical=661, title=off, effects-        background=off.
10. For HIGH Consumption Text Box --> In the format pane->general:
   Properties: Height=32, width=200, horizontal=1080,vertical=661, title=off, effects-background=off.

Insight: 

Create Map Visual

Global Fossil Fuel Production and Consumption - Power BI Dashboard
Do you know that: The Last Decade Was the Hottest in 125,000 Years? The 10 warmest years in the historical record have all occurred since 2010? Lets find out why... Fossil fuels are formed from the decomposition of buried carbon-based organisms that died millions of years ago. They create carbon-rich deposits that are extracted and burned for energy. They are non-renewable and currently supply around 80% of the world’s energy. They been a fundamental driver of the technological, social, economic and development progress. Fossil fuels (coal, oil, gas) have, and continue to, play a dominant role in global energy systems. But they also come with several negative impacts. When burned they produce carbon dioxide (CO2) and are the largest driver of global climate change. They are also a major contributor to local air pollution, which is estimated to linked to millions of premature deaths each year. As low-carbon sources of energy – nuclear and renewables – become readily available, the world needs to rapidly transition away from fossil fuels. This projects provides detailed insights on fossil fuel production and consumption across the globe. This report will help identify the countries that are major consumers of fossil fuel which should take steps to move towards the renewable energy sources like wind, solar, nuclear fusion to help minimize the global warming and pollution to make the earth a better place to live.
Step Name:Final Dashboard

portfolioContent.title: Global Fossil Fuel Production and Consumption - Power BI Dashboard
portfolioContent.portfolioSummary: Developed a Power BI dashboard analyzing global fossil fuel production and consumption to support energy transition decisions.
portfolioContent.summary: This project created a comprehensive Power BI dashboard presenting detailed insights into global fossil fuel production and consumption patterns. It highlights major consumer countries and the environmental impacts of fossil fuel use. The dashboard supports data-driven decision-making for transitioning to renewable energy sources.
portfolioContent.businessProblem: Fossil fuels supply around 80% of the world’s energy but are the largest contributors to global climate change and local air pollution. There is an urgent need to identify key fossil fuel consumers to promote a shift towards low-carbon energy sources. The project addresses the challenge of visualizing complex energy data to inform policy and business strategies.
objectives: Analyze global fossil fuel production and consumption data
Identify major fossil fuel consuming countries
Support transition strategies towards renewable energy
workflow: Collect and clean global fossil fuel production and consumption data
Perform ETL to prepare data for analysis
Develop interactive Power BI dashboard visualizations
Analyze data to identify key fossil fuel consumers and trends
Present insights to support renewable energy transition decisions
keyInsights: Fossil fuels remain the dominant energy source globally, supplying about 80% of energy needs
The last decade was the hottest in 125,000 years, linked to fossil fuel emissions
Major fossil fuel consumers have significant roles in global climate change
Transitioning to renewables like wind, solar, and nuclear is critical to reduce pollution and warming
businessImpact: Enabled identification of countries critical for fossil fuel reduction efforts
Provided actionable insights to support energy policy and sustainability initiatives
Facilitated data-driven decisions for accelerating renewable energy adoption
